title: carya texana f. glabra
intro: Carya texana f. glabra, also known as the smooth pecan, is a deciduous tree native to the southern United States, belonging to the Juglandaceae family. The bark and leaves of the smooth pecan have been used in traditional medicine for their antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which contribute to its medicinal value. The health benefits of smooth pecan include its potential to reduce oxidative stress, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit antidiabetic properties, making it a valuable herbal remedy. Smooth pecan can be prepared as a tincture, tea, or infused oil, which can be used to treat various health conditions, and it is also used in traditional folk medicine to treat fever, sore throats, and skin conditions.
